summary_one_line: A syntactically valid SELECT statement with a dotted column-expression list that drives the yy_reduce reduction at 157646's true side.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: An input whose leading bytes spell the SELECT keyword followed by expression tokens (dotted-list / column-reference structure), reaching the deep expression-parse reduction in yy_reduce at 157646.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: value_profile winner seeds begin with the literal bytes 53 45 4c 45 43 54 20 ('SELECT ') and continue with dotted-list expression tokens, so they drive resolveExprStep (15 fires in W, 0 in L) and hit the blocker true side 11 times.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: naive loser-blocking seeds do not start with SELECT (they begin with 'T ', backticks, 'E', ';'), never enter the expression-resolution path, and take the blocker false side 61/61.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: value_profile's CMP_MAP records operand-distance (matching-prefix length) for the tokenizer/keyword integer and string CMPs; an input one byte closer to spelling SELECT and the subsequent expression tokens adds a new CMP_MAP bucket and is preserved, so VP ratchets bytes toward the valid SELECT-expression prefix that naive cannot assemble by blind havoc.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: An input that reaches the yy_reduce reducer at 157646 via the deep SELECT-parse call context (sqlite3RunParser -> sqlite3Parser -> yy_reduce), so the edge is newly interesting under context-indexed coverage.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: naive_ctx winner seeds begin with 'SELECT' (53 45 4c 45 43 54) and drive yy_reduce to hit the blocker true side 11 times with avg hitcount 140 on the branch, far above the loser's 3.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: naive loser seeds resolve the branch in only 2/10 trials with avg hitcount 3, never sustaining the deep parse call path that would make the reducer edge a fresh (call-context, edge) coverage event.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: naive_ctx installs a CtxHook that folds the running caller-chain hash into the edge-map index, so the yy_reduce reducer edge reached through the SELECT-expression call chain registers as a new (call-context, edge) bucket; this keeps intermediate SELECT-shaped inputs in the corpus and lets naive_ctx incrementally extend the parse where bare-edge naive coverage sees nothing new.

evidence_trail.claim: The two decisive pairs differ by distinct techniques: value_profile and ctx_coverage.
The second decisive pair is the ctx_coverage delta (naive_ctx beats naive).
The blocked side is the true branch inside the yy_reduce parser reducer.
value_profile winner seeds begin with the literal bytes for 'SELECT '.
naive_ctx winner seeds also begin with the SELECT keyword.
Winner inputs all carry SELECT at offsets 0x00-0x05 while loser inputs carry non-SELECT bytes there.
Loser-blocking seeds do not begin with SELECT (e.g. start with 'T ').
Winner inputs drive expression resolution (resolveExprStep) that loser inputs never reach.
At the blocker line the winner takes the true side 11 times while the loser never does.
The ctx_coverage winner shows a far larger branch hitcount than the loser.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: avg hitcount on branch: winner=140  loser=3

evidence_trail.claim: value_profile rewards inputs whose CMP operands move closer to matching via the CMP_MAP distance bucket.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
An input that produces a
CMP-operand pair closer to matching than any previously-seen pair
adds a new CMP_MAP bucket and is preserved as corpus.

evidence_trail.claim: naive_ctx folds the call-context hash into the edge index so the same edge counts as new coverage on a new call path.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
folds it into the edge-map index, so the same basic-block edge is
recorded at different map slots depending on the call path that
reached it.

mechanism_consistency_check.verification_method: Lineage is not mandatory here (claimed mechanisms are CMP_MAP gradient and context-sensitive coverage, not I2SRandReplace). I ran `python3 tools/db_query.py lineage --branch 14745 --role W --fuzzer value_profile --trial 1 --seed bf9a426c1bd27d40` for corroboration: it reported one dash row at depth 15, but the MECHANISM CONTEXT states value_profile has no I2S stage and dash rows are characteristic of cmplog/vpc, so I treat that as a leaked-havoc-bucket artifact and do NOT attribute the win to I2SRandReplace. The multi_feature consistency cross-check is satisfied per-hypothesis: Pair 1 = CMP_MAP gradient, Pair 2 = context-sensitive coverage.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness where the blocker reduction can be reached by a non-SELECT input (no keyword/expression prefix dataflow) yet value_profile still resolves it significantly faster than naive — showing the win is not driven by CMP_MAP ratcheting toward the SELECT-expression prefix.

weakest_evidence_point: Whether the divergence is driven by tokenizer/keyword CMP feedback specifically (CMP_MAP gradient) versus generic deep-parse difficulty is inferred from the SELECT-prefix byte pattern, not from a named CMP comparison in the source, since the blocker source text was not available.
